steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what point the text makes about the difference in park use between Quito and Lima. The key sentence is: "greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use." That directly tells us the cause is something other than how close residents live to parks.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It was much larger than the researchers conducting the study expected.': The text never mentions researcher expectations. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It could be due to inaccuracies in the survey results.': The text presents the survey data as straightforward fact, not as potentially flawed. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It was calculated using sources that predate the survey.': The text says nothing about the age or origin of the data sources. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents.': This matches exactly — the text explicitly states that proximity alone can't explain the difference, implying another cause. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
